title: Fresh Filipino Fried Lechon
description: A light and earthy take on the classic Filipino dish, featuring a sous-vide twist and minimal ingredients.
instructions: Preheat the sous vide machine to 160 6.
Season the pork belly with salt and black pepper.
In a blender or food processor, blend the madras paste, garlic, ginger, and anchovies until smooth.
Rub the blended mixture all over the pork belly, making sure to coat it evenly.
Sous-vide the pork belly for 24 hours.
After 24 hours, remove the pork belly from the sous-vide machine and let it rest at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Heat the coconut oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Sear the pork belly until crispy and golden brown on both sides, about 5-7 minutes per side.
Transfer the pork belly to a cutting board and let it rest for 5 minutes before slicing.
Slice the pork belly into thin strips and serve with steamed rice, a sprinkle of fish sauce, and a drizzle of lime juice, garnished with fresh cilantro and fried shallots.
